stories.title: Spurs Stun Thunder in Game 7, Advance to NBA Finals

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
SPURS WIN GAME 7.

SA 111, OKC 103. Wemby 22/7. Champagnie 20 on six threes. Castle 16/6/6. Seven Spurs in double figures.

SGA had 35 pts, 9 ast. The Thunder had only SGA.

The Wemby era is Finals-bound. 🏀

#Spurs #NBAPlayoffs

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
The Spurs went into OKC as underdogs and won Game 7 on the road.

Jalen Williams was out. SGA dropped 35. None of it mattered.

Seven Spurs in double digits. Champagnie hit six threes.

San Antonio is going to the NBA Finals. #Spurs #NBAPlayoffs

facebook.long_form:
The San Antonio Spurs are going to the NBA Finals.

In one of the most stunning road performances of this postseason, the Spurs walked into Paycom Center — without the crowd on their side, without the betting line in their favor — and delivered a complete 111-103 Game 7 victory over the Oklahoma City Thunder.

Victor Wembanyama led the way with 22 points and seven rebounds across 42 grueling minutes. But this wasn't a one-man show. It never is with the Spurs. Julian Champagnie was unconscious from deep, pouring in 20 points on six three-pointers that silenced the OKC crowd one dagger at a time. Stephon Castle added 16 points, six rebounds, and six assists. De'Aaron Fox chipped in 15. Seven San Antonio players scored in double figures.

On the other side, Shai Gilgeous-Alexander gave everything he had — 35 points, nine assists, two steals, two blocks in a losing effort. But with Jalen Williams sidelined by his hamstring injury, SGA simply couldn't do it alone.

San Antonio advances to the NBA Finals for the first time since 2014. A new era of Spurs basketball has officially arrived — and it's led by a 22-year-old with a 7-foot wingspan and generational talent.

The Knicks are waiting. Game 1 is Wednesday, June 3 in San Antonio on ABC.

Who do you have? 👇
facebook.image_caption: Seven Spurs scorers. One unstoppable team. San Antonio is Finals-bound. 🏀 #Spurs #NBAPlayoffs

article.headline: Wemby and the Spurs Stun Thunder in Game 7, Advance to NBA Finals

stories.title: NBA Finals Set — Knicks vs. Spurs (1999 Rematch)

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text:
IT'S OFFICIAL: Knicks vs. Spurs — NBA Finals 2026.

G1: Wed June 3, 8:30 PM ET, ABC. At San Antonio.

The last time these two met in the Finals? 1999. Spurs won.

Brunson vs. Wemby. Let's go.

#Knicks #Spurs #NBAFinals

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text:
Knicks-Spurs Finals odds:

Spurs: -196 (home court, 62-20)
Knicks: +164 (11-game win streak, ECF sweep)

Brunson vs. Wemby. MSG vs. AT&T Center. 27 years in the making.

Game 1: June 3, ABC. 🏆 #NBAFinals #Knicks #Spurs

facebook.long_form:
Let's just say it out loud: this is the NBA Finals we've been waiting for.

The New York Knicks vs. the San Antonio Spurs. Game 1 is Wednesday, June 3 at 8:30 PM ET on ABC — in San Antonio, where the Spurs enter as -196 favorites.

This isn't just basketball. This is history. Twenty-seven years ago, in 1999, the Spurs and Knicks met in the NBA Finals. San Antonio won in five games. The Spurs were led by Tim Duncan. The Knicks were gritty underdogs who shocked the world just by getting there.

Now it's 2026. The Spurs are still the Spurs — different stars, same DNA — but this time they're led by Victor Wembanyama, who just earned WCF MVP honors. The Knicks are still the Knicks — different jerseys, same city hunger — but Jalen Brunson has made MSG electric all postseason with 27.8 PPG and an 11-game winning streak.

The Spurs hold home court at the AT&T Center (62-20 regular season). The Knicks have MSG for Games 3 and 4. Both cities will be electric.

Who wins the 2026 NBA Championship? Drop your prediction below. 👇

Schedule:
🏀 G1: June 3 — ABC — at San Antonio
🏀 G2: June 5 — ABC — at San Antonio
🏀 G3: June 8 — ABC — at New York
🏀 G4: June 10 — ABC — at New York
facebook.image_caption: Knicks vs. Spurs. 27 years in the making. Who's taking home the trophy? 🏆 #NBAFinals #Knicks #Spurs

article.headline: Knicks vs. Spurs: The 2026 NBA Finals Is a 27-Year Rematch

stories.title: Wembanyama Named WCF MVP — Historic Postseason Run

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
Victor Wembanyama: 2026 WCF MVP.

Series: 27.3 PPG / 10.9 RPG / 2.7 BPG / 1.4 SPG

He's 22 years old. Already breaking Tim Duncan's Spurs postseason records.

This is only the beginning. #Spurs #NBAPlayoffs

facebook.long_form:
Victor Wembanyama has been named the 2026 Western Conference Finals MVP — and the numbers tell you everything you need to know.

Over seven games against the defending champion Oklahoma City Thunder, Wemby averaged 27.3 points, 10.9 rebounds, 3.1 assists, 2.7 blocks, and 1.4 steals per game. He played 37.7 minutes per night and never seemed to get tired.

He's 22 years old. He's already breaking Tim Duncan's Spurs postseason records. And the best part? He's just getting started.

Game 7 felt like a microcosm of who Wemby is as a player. He didn't need to go off for 40 to win — he scored 22 efficiently, hauled in seven rebounds, and let his teammates shine. Julian Champagnie, Stephon Castle, De'Aaron Fox — they all had moments. But the gravitational pull of Wembanyama created every one of those openings.

This is what elite two-way basketball looks like. This is what the future of the NBA looks like.

And now he has to face the New York Knicks in the Finals. On June 3. On ABC.

Don't miss it.
facebook.image_caption: WCF MVP. 27.3 PPG. 10.9 RPG. 2.7 BPG. And he's 22. Victor Wembanyama is just getting started. 🏆 #Wemby #Spurs #NBAPlayoffs

article.headline: Wembanyama Named WCF MVP: A Historic Postseason at Age 22

stories.title: SGA's 35-Point Night Falls Short — Thunder Exit in Game 7

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
SGA: 35 pts, 9 ast in a Game 7 elimination loss.

He did everything. His team ran out of answers without Jalen Williams.

"They were just the better team tonight, from start to finish." — SGA

Back-to-back MVP. Respect. #Thunder #NBAPlayoffs

facebook.long_form:
Shai Gilgeous-Alexander gave everything he had. It just wasn't enough.

In Game 7 of the Western Conference Finals, the back-to-back MVP dropped 35 points, nine assists, four rebounds, two steals, and two blocks — a performance that, on most nights, wins basketball games. But the San Antonio Spurs had seven players reach double figures, and without Jalen Williams (out with a hamstring injury), the Thunder simply ran out of answers.

When it was over, SGA didn't make excuses. He faced the cameras and was honest: "They were just the better team tonight, from start to finish."

That's class. And when asked about the Thunder's offseason plans, he gave the answer of a franchise player who trusts his organization completely: "I will give zero input. I will let Sam Presti, the greatest GM ever, do his job."

Two straight MVP awards. A team that was legitimate championship contenders for back-to-back years. The 2025 championship banner still hangs in OKC. This is not a failure — this is the foundation of something that could run for years.

SGA will be back. Oklahoma City will be back.

But tonight belongs to San Antonio.
facebook.image_caption: 35 points. 9 assists. All heart. SGA gave everything in Game 7. The future is still bright in OKC. 💪 #Thunder #SGA #NBAPlayoffs

article.headline: SGA Dropped 35 in Game 7 and It Still Wasn't Enough

stories.title: The 1999 Rematch — Brunson's Father Played for Those Knicks

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
In 1999, Rick Brunson played for the Knicks when they lost the Finals to the Spurs.

Now his son Jalen is leading the Knicks back — against the same Spurs.

27 years later. Same two teams. Different Brunsons.

This storyline writes itself. #Knicks #NBAFinals

facebook.long_form:
Sometimes sports write the most beautiful stories.

In 1999, the New York Knicks made a miraculous run to the NBA Finals as a No. 8 seed. They were gritty, tough, and represented New York with everything they had. They lost to the San Antonio Spurs in five games. On the roster that year was a reserve guard named Rick Brunson — a journeyman who worked his way into the league and played his part for the Knicks.

That reserve guard had a son named Jalen.

Twenty-seven years later, Jalen Brunson — who grew up in Union, New Jersey, who watched his father play for the Knicks, who signed with New York from Dallas in 2022 and immediately became the face of a franchise — has led those same New York Knicks back to the NBA Finals. Against the same San Antonio Spurs.

Jalen Brunson averaged 27.8 PPG and 6.7 APG through this playoff run. He was named ECF MVP after the Knicks swept the Cavaliers. And when he was asked about being in the Finals, he said: "It's an honor to be here in this city and this organization. I wouldn't trade it for the world. We're still writing our story, but I like the journey that we're on right now."

Rick Brunson lost that 1999 Finals to the Spurs.

Now it's Jalen's turn to rewrite the ending.

Game 1: June 3, 8:30 PM ET, ABC.
facebook.image_caption: Rick Brunson played for the 1999 Knicks who lost the Finals to the Spurs. Now his son Jalen is bringing NYC back — against the Spurs. 27 years later. 🗽 #Knicks #NBAFinals

article.headline: His Father Lost the 1999 Finals to the Spurs. Now Jalen Brunson Gets His Shot.
